title: Work & School
shortSummary: Talk about your job, your studies, and your daily working life in Italian.
culturalNote: When Italians ask che lavoro fai? (what work do you do?), it opens an easy, friendly conversation. Job titles often change with gender, so a male teacher is un insegnante and the work itself is teaching; many professions add a feminine form (un dottore, una dottoressa). The working day frequently includes a real lunch break, and the institution of the long coffee chat with colleagues is part of office culture. Students talk about la scuola (school) and l'università (university), and exams (gli esami) are often oral as well as written.

grammar.rule: Jobs without an article after essere
grammar.explanation: When stating a profession after essere, Italian often drops the article: sono medico, sono insegnante. The article appears only when you add a description: sono un bravo medico.
grammar.commonMistake: Always inserting un or una. Plain professions usually go without: faccio l'avvocato or sono avvocato.

grammar.explanation: Some -ire verbs add -isc- in most present forms: finire (to finish) gives finisco, finisci, finisce, finiamo, finite, finiscono. Capire (to understand) and preferire work the same way.
grammar.commonMistake: Forgetting the -isc- and saying fino instead of finisco.

dialogue.setup: Two people chat at a networking event.

lines.speaker: Davide
lines.english: Nice to meet you, Davide. What work do you do?

lines.speaker: Davide
lines.native: Sì, molto. Finisco sempre alle sei.
lines.english: Yes, a lot. I always finish at six.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.native: Comodo! Io a volte lavoro da casa.
lines.english: Convenient! I sometimes work from home.

lines.speaker: Davide
lines.native: Anche questo è un bel vantaggio.

quiz.question: How do you ask 'What work do you do?' in Italian?
quiz.explanation: Che lavoro fai? is the standard informal question.

quiz.question: Which is the correct io form of finire?
What does lavoro da casa mean?
Arrange: lavoro / mio / il / piace / mi
quiz.explanation: Mi piace il mio lavoro means 'I like my job'.
